message:
feat(input): add C1 control code support for CSI/SS3/OSC sequences; fix synchronized output teardown ownership

C1 control code parsing:
- Handle 8-bit C1 CSI (0x9B), SS3 (0x8F), and OSC (0x9D) byte values
  as direct sequence introducers without requiring the ESC prefix,
  supporting terminals that use S8C1T (Send 8-bit C1 Transmission) mode
- CSI (0x9B) transitions to CsiState and clears the buffer identically
  to ESC [ for correct parameter parsing
- SS3 (0x8F) transitions to Ss3State for function key handling
- OSC (0x9D) transitions to OscState for operating system commands
- Add tests for C1 CSI arrow keys and C1 CSI SGR mouse protocol parsing

Synchronized output teardown fix:
- Remove standalone DEC ?2026l (SYNC_END) emission from
  TerminalSession::cleanup() and TtyBackend::Drop — the renderer/writer
  owns synchronized output bracketing and closes it within frame writes
- Remove SYNC_END from TtyBackend error cleanup path since no sync block
  is opened during setup
- Prevents orphaned mode-reset sequences from reaching the terminal
  outside an active bracket, which caused visual glitches in some
  multiplexer configurations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
